log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader with runtime modules, packed with ASPack and an overlay. Investigate the network activity to see if it communicates with any command and control ser
The malware does not appear to have any network activity, which is unusual for a spreader. Investigate the registry changes to see if it attempts to persist on the system.

The malware attempts to persist on the system by modifying the Winlogon\Shell registry key to run HelpMe.exe. Investigate the dropped files to see if HelpMe.exe is a significant component of the malwa

The malware drops a large number of files, including HelpMe.exe, which is likely a significant component of the malware. Investigate the process activity to see if HelpMe.exe is executed and what othe

HelpMe.exe is executed and loads several notable modules, including API libraries and system components. Investigate the CAPA behaviors to see if there are any suspicious or malicious behaviors associ

The malware exhibits several suspicious behaviors, including being packed with ASPack and containing a thread local storage section. It also references the startup folder, which is consistent with its
The malware only has one persistence mechanism, which is modifying the Winlogon\Shell registry key. This is consistent with the hypothesis that it uses the startup folder for persistence.
